name: Puig del Àguila — circular por la Sierra de Cogullada
region_intro: Jogging in Parc D'Olèrdola offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, rocky outcrops, and Mediterranean forests. The region features varied terrain, including ancient historical sites and natural viewpoints, making it suitable for different running preferences. Many circular jogging trails Parc D'Olèrdola provide opportunities to explore its natural beauty and cultural heritage. The elevation changes across the park's routes offer both challenging ascents and gentler sections. Best jogging routes in Parc D'Olèrdola The most popular jogging route is Puig del Àguila — circular por la Sierra de Cogullada, a 7.2 miles (11.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route features steady climbs and panoramic views from Puig del Àguila. Another top favourite among local runners is Castillo de Sant Miquel d'Olèrdola — circular por las rocas de Can Ximet, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This route winds through historical sites and rocky landscapes. Local runners also love the Puig de l'Àguila – Turó de les Tres Partions loop from Canyelles, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail leading through hilly terrain and natural viewpoints, often completed in about 1 hour 33 minutes. Jogging in Parc D'Olèrdola is defined by its historical landmarks, rocky paths, and dense woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult ascents. The routes in Parc D'Olèrdola are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 10,000 runners have used komoot to explore Parc D'Olèrdola's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Parc D'Olèrdola?
A: Parc D'Olèrdola offers a wide selection of over 400 running routes. These range from easy paths to more challenging trails, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Parc D'Olèrdola?
A: Yes, Parc D'Olèrdola has 32 easy running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a gentler pace. These paths typically feature less elevation gain and smoother terrain.

Q: Are there circular jogging trails in Parc D'Olèrdola?
A: Many of the running routes in Parc D'Olèrdola are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1118873948/castillo-de-sant-miquel-d-olerdola-circular-por-las-rocas-de-can-ximet" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Sant Miquel d'Olèrdola — circular por las rocas de Can Ximet&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints along the running routes?
A: Runners can explore several historical sites and enjoy panoramic views. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2579854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de l'Àliga&lt;/a&gt; summit, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3922796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olèrdola Castle and Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3922795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pla dels Albats Necropolis and Chapel Ruins&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23777427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de l'Àguila – Turó de les Tres Partions loop from Canyelles&lt;/a&gt; offers excellent natural viewpoints.

Q: Are the running trails in Parc D'Olèrdola suitable for families?
A: While many routes feature varied terrain, there are easier paths that families can enjoy. Look for routes marked as 'easy' in the komoot app, which typically have shorter distances and less challenging elevation changes, making them more suitable for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Parc D'Olèrdola?
A: Yes, Parc D'Olèrdola is generally dog-friendly, and many trails are suitable for running with your canine companion. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near historical sites, and to check local regulations for specific areas within the park.

Q: What is the typical length of running trails in Parc D'Olèrdola?
A: Running trails in Parc D'Olèrdola vary significantly in length. You can find shorter routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14901251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wikipedra mur 107 loop from Canyelles&lt;/a&gt; at around 3.3 miles (5.3 km), up to longer, more challenging runs such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131419944/puig-del-aguila-circular-por-la-sierra-de-cogullada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig del Àguila — circular por la Sierra de Cogullada&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 7.2 miles (11.5 km).

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Parc D'Olèrdola?
A: The running routes in Parc D'Olèrdola are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 100 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscape, which includes historical sites, rocky paths, and dense woodlands, as well as the panoramic views from elevated points like Puig del Àguila.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the jogging trails?
A: While the park itself is primarily natural and historical, there are small villages and towns surrounding Parc D'Olèrdola, such as Canyelles and Olèrdola, where you can find local cafes and restaurants to refuel after your run. It's advisable to check opening hours, especially outside peak season.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Parc D'Olèrdola?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for running in Parc D'Olèrdola, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended. Winter running is also possible, though some paths might be damp after rain.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Parc D'Olèrdola?
A: Parking is typically available at various access points and villages surrounding Parc D'Olèrdola. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route pages within komoot or by checking local municipality websites for designated parking areas near popular trailheads.

Q: Are there any running routes that lead to a summit?
A: Yes, several routes offer the reward of reaching a summit with expansive views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38261715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de l'Àliga Summit loop from Canyelles&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for those looking to conquer a peak and enjoy the vistas.
tips: Nice pla del Parc d'Olèrdola with very good views and a lot of Romani (Romero)

The Puig de l'Àliga is a mountain located in the last foothills to the south of the Garraf massif, between the municipalities of Canyelles and Olèrdola, the latter in the Alt Penedès region.

The summit is surrounded by cliffs, but it is a wide ridge. At the highest point we find the geodesic vertex, a few meters from a fire watch tower. On the northeast side, in addition to good views of Montserrat, we will find a large cross. And, a little further down, a great picnic area / viewpoint.

The south face, however, faces the sea and due to its position it enjoys a great panoramic view towards the coast. Therefore, it is a recommended point to contemplate the Mediterranean sunrise.
An abric is a balma-shaped rock formation that has the shape of a cliff and is named after the nearby farm/house. The path that crosses it is beautiful with good views of the valley below.
